How to keep the map useful, honest, and player-first.

Map Leaderboard
Sign in
Guidelines

Community Guidelines

Contribute the kind of open-play details you would want before driving to a court.

Last updated: June 15, 2026

Submit real open play

Add places where players can reasonably expect to find pickup games, open play sessions, or recurring community play. Do not add a court just because it has pickleball lines if there is no open play information to share.

Make information useful

  • Include the park or facility name, city, state, accurate pin location, and open play days or time windows when you know them.
  • Share practical details: skill level, crowd size, net setup, fees, lighting, bathrooms, water, parking, paddle stack, and beginner friendliness.
  • Explain how current the information is, especially if schedules change seasonally or depend on weather.
  • Use suggested edits when information has changed instead of creating duplicate locations.

Write honest reviews

Reviews should be based on your real court experience or a good-faith note from a recent visit. Keep reviews focused on details that help players decide where and when to play. Do not post fake reviews, paid reviews, retaliation, personal attacks, or secondhand rumors presented as fact.

Upload responsible photos

  • Only upload photos you took or have permission to share.
  • Good photos show courts, lines, nets, lighting, parking, signage, or facility setup.
  • Avoid close-up images of people who did not agree to be featured, especially children.
  • Do not upload private information, license plates as the focus of the image, explicit content, harassment, ads, screenshots, or unrelated images.

Respect people and places

Do not harass players, facility staff, admins, or other users. Do not post personal information about someone else. Follow posted facility rules, local laws, and staff instructions. If a location asks that certain information or photos be removed for safety, privacy, or operational reasons, report it.

No spam or reward manipulation

Credits are meant to reward helpful contributions. Do not submit duplicate locations, low-effort reviews, fake photos, copied content, misleading reports, or multiple accounts to gain credits. We may remove content, adjust credits, or restrict accounts when contributions hurt the quality of the map.

Report problems

Use report tools or contact scooppickleball@gmail.com if you see incorrect location details, unsafe content, privacy concerns, copyright issues, duplicate listings, or abuse.
